news.headline: Akazawa will continue discussions on car tariffs
Akazawa does not comment on negotiations details

news.summary: South Korea-based Fation, a leading skincare brand known for it's trouble-care products, has introduced its highly popular line at Olive Young stores in the U.S. The brand has received an impressive rating of 4.9 out of 5.0 from consumers. The new product, Nosca9 Deep, is the next hit after the success of the 'Trouble' Line.
news.headline: Korean skincare brand Fation launches in the U.S. after success in Korea with top-rated products. 4.9/5 rating at Olive Young.
Yen weakens against dollar as Japan Akazawa speaks
Yen weakens by 0.3% to 142.27 for each dollar

news.summary: Advent Technologies Holdings, Inc., based in Livermore, California, and listed on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol ADN, disclosed today that it has been notified by Nasdaq on April 16, 2025, regarding non-compliance with periodic filing requirements outlined in Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250(c)(1). The basis for this notice is the delay in filing the Company’s Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024. The report was due by March 31, 2025, and as of the notification date, it had not been submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission.
news.headline: Advent Technologies Holdings received Nasdaq notice for late Form 10-K filing by March 31, 2025, on April 16, 2025.

news.summary: In Tucson, Arizona, Mister Car Wash, Inc. (Nasdaq: MCW), the largest car wash brand in the U.S., revealed that its financial outcomes for Q1 of 2025 will be disclosed post-market closing on Wednesday, April 30, 2025. Alongside, a conference call will be led by the company at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time on the same day to deliberate on the financial results. Those keen to join the call, including investors and analysts, can dial 855-209-8213 (international code not provided) for participation.
news.headline: Mister Car Wash to release Q1 2025 financial results on April 30, 2025. Conference call at 4:30pm ET. Dial 855-209-8213 to participate.

news.summary: In the most recent report, it was disclosed that the unemployment rate in the country has reached 6.2%, with a total of 10.1 million people currently unemployed. This marks a slight decrease from the previous month. Additionally, the report showed that there was a rise in job creation, with 280,000 new jobs added to the economy, mainly in the service and manufacturing sectors. Analysts predict that this trend is likely to continue in the coming months as the economy gradually recovers from the impact of the pandemic. The government has also announced plans to introduce new stimulus measures to further boost economic growth and job creation.
news.headline: Federal homelessness agency places all staff on leave. DOGE-affected employees left job. #news #federalagency #DOGE
Schmid: US will need artificial intelligence to remain productive given demographics
Japan sold foreign bonds last week during US turmoil: Ministry of Finance
Japanese Foreign Stock Investment Actual 258.1B (Forecast -, Previous 1,796.1B)

news.summary: After President Trump issued a delay on implementing tariffs on Mexico, the S&P 500 rebounded from its previous lows. This move underscores the ambitious nature of his trade strategies. The postponement of the tariffs on Mexican goods came after the market's initial negative response, signaling potential risk associated with the plan. However, tariffs on Chinese imports are still scheduled to be imposed. This development comes amidst discussions of the impact of Trump's trade policies on the financial markets.
news.headline: Trump delays Mexico tariffs as S&P 500 falls due to high-risk plan, China tariffs unchanged. Market reacts boldly.

news.summary: Inflation expectations increased after a somewhat hawkish jobs report, impacting the likelihood of a Fed rate cut. This led to a decline in the S&P 500 as investors reacted to the news. The rise in inflation expectations and the content of the jobs report influenced the probabilities of a Fed rate cut, creating uncertainty in the market.
news.headline: Hawkish jobs report & inflation expectations affect Fed rate-cut odds; S&P 500 falls.

news.summary: New data from the producer price index indicates that inflation is not as severe as previously indicated by the consumer price index. Despite this, S&P 500 futures are increasing. However, the Federal Reserve is expected to maintain its current stance due to concerns about the impact of Trump's tariffs. The latest PPI report shows that inflation continues to decline, providing some relief amidst fears driven by the CPI results. The S&P 500 is on the rise following this news.
news.headline: Producer Price Index (PPI) reveals decreasing inflation despite CPI scare; S&P 500 futures increase. Trump tariffs may impact Fed's decision.

news.summary: Tariffs imposed by the Trump administration at a rate of 25% are expected to impact three key industries with significant trade deficits by April 2. This additional round of tariffs adds to the three existing tariff deadlines. The affected industries are facing substantial challenges ahead as a result of these trade policies.
news.headline: Trump is targeting industries with big trade deficits with 25% tariffs by April 2, adding to 3 other upcoming tariff deadlines.

news.summary: Amidst Trump's tariff threats causing turbulence in the market, the S&P 500 experienced fluctuations while the Federal Reserve's primary inflation indicator, the PCE price index, showed signs of cooling. Spending slowed down, contributing to the economic landscape. This development offers some hope and a clearer perspective on the current situation. The S&P 500 saw a rise amidst these uncertainties, showcasing the market's resilience even in the face of potential disturbances.
news.headline: Fed's PCE price index drops as spending slows, S&P 500 rises despite Trump tariff concerns.

news.summary: Last month, planned layoffs saw a significant increase, reaching a four-year peak, attributed to the influence of Elon Musk's DOGE. However, amidst this surge, it was reported that former President Trump no longer holds the power to enact job cuts.
news.headline: Planned layoffs hit a four-year high due to Elon Musk's DOGE, while Trump stopped the surge.

news.summary: In February, there was only a small increase in employment, accompanied by the beginning of a significant number of job losses at Department of General Employment (DOGE) and the implementation of tariffs by former President Trump. The labor market appeared to be weakening according to the latest jobs report. However, Federal Reserve Chair Powell described it as stable. Despite this, the S&P 500 index rebounded during the month.
news.headline: February jobs report reveals modest hiring, doge job cuts, and Trump tariffs; Fed Chair Powell views labor market as 'solid'. S&P 500 rebounds.

news.summary: In February, the consumer price index revealed that core inflation decreased more than anticipated, reflecting consumer hesitancy. S&P 500 futures saw a significant increase in response. The decline in inflation is attributed to a decrease in travel following Trump's policies. The news was originally reported by Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: CPI inflation drops as consumer caution rises, S&P 500 futures soar.

news.summary: The recent layoffs at DOGE have not yet reflected in the initial claims for unemployment benefits. Despite this, there is concern about a potential shutdown. The S&P 500 experienced a decline in response to these developments.
news.headline: DOGE layoffs not impacting jobless claims; shutdown risk looms for S&P 500 as index slips.

news.summary: Jerome Powell, the Federal Reserve Chairman, is seeking a better understanding of the Trump administration's plans before considering a rate cut. However, he may not have the time to wait for clarity. Investors are eager to hear Powell's insights at the upcoming Fed meeting to determine the course for the S&P 500. Powell's decision on whether or not to cut rates will be crucial for the market, which is anxiously anticipating a prompt response from the chairman.
news.headline: Fed Chair Powell awaits clarity on Trump agenda before rate cut, S&P 500 hopeful but uncertain.

news.summary: Members of the Federal Reserve anticipate a total of two interest rate reductions to occur in the year 2025. This decision aligns with an environment marked by a rise in the unemployment rate and a temporary period of inflation due to tariffs. Despite this economic outlook, characterized as dovish by analysts, the S&P 500 experienced a surge in response to the news, indicating positive investor sentiment toward the Fed's stance on promoting slow growth and managing inflation.
news.headline: Fed expects 2 rate cuts in 2025, hints at inflation as S&P 500 surges; higher unemployment noted.

news.summary: In February, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure increased. This comes as the key inflation rate grew before the implementation of Trump's tariffs. As a result, S&P 500 futures experienced a decline.
news.headline: Inflation rate rises before Trump tariffs; S&P 500 futures slip. Fed's gauge increased in Feb. Source: Investor's Business Daily.

news.summary: In a surprising turn of events, consumer prices experienced a decrease, with core inflation reaching its lowest point in four years. The decline in Consumer Price Index (CPI) was unexpected, influenced by the impact of Trump tariffs on travel expenses. This resulted in a notable drop in the S&P 500, reflecting the uncertainty in the market.
news.headline: Consumer prices fell unexpectedly, core inflation at a four-year low; Trump tariffs sink travel, S&P 500 falls sharply.

news.summary: In Jacksonville, Florida, Dun & Bradstreet (NYSE:DNB), a top provider of business data and analytics, has launched a free supply chain assessment tool. This tool gives businesses insights into risks and opportunities within their supply chains. By utilizing Dun & Bradstreet's exclusive data, the evaluation highlights the financial impact of anticipated tariffs on a company's supply chain. This initiative aims to help businesses better understand their supply chain dynamics and make informed decisions.
news.headline: Dun & Bradstreet provides free supply chain evaluation for businesses to understand risks, analyze expenses due to tariffs.
